View Edit History Print TockOS / Compiling Duktape 2.x for the Hail Board under TockOS Duktape 2.x was released in early 2017, so we should use Duktape 2.x for the Hail board and TockOS instead of Duktape1.x This page assumes that the Hail board has

ktape1.x This page assumes that the Hail board has been updated with the latest TockOS . See Hail for details. Repo Check out my fork, build TockOS and an app Check out the branch git clone https: // github.com / cxbrooks / tock.git cd tock git chec

github.com / cxbrooks / tock.git cd tock git checkout cxbrooks / duktape4 [$[Get Code]] Build and install TockOS. Note that newer versions of tockloader do not need --port : cd boards / hail make TOCK_BOARD =hail program [$[Get Code]] Build and ins

CK_BOARD =hail program [$[Get Code]] Build and install an example application: cd .. / .. / userland / examples / accel-leds / make TOCK_BOARD =hail program [$[Get Code]] Use tockloader list : bash-3.2$ tockloader list No serial port specified. Disco

2$ tockloader list No serial port specified. Discovering attached serial devices... Using "/dev/cu.usbserial-00001014 - Hail IoT Module - TockOS - Hail IoT Module - TockOS" [ App 0 ] Name: accel-leds Total Size in Flash: 4096 bytes b

accel-leds Total Size in Flash: 4096 bytes bash-3.2$ [$[Get Code]] Simple See Duktape2 Simple for a simple JavaScript test Toggle a LED See Duktape2 Toggle LED RampJSDisplay See Duktape2 RampJSDisplay for a simple accessor Update my fork To upd

isplay for a simple accessor Update my fork To update the https://github.com/cxbrooks/tock with recent changes in https://github.com/helena-project/tock: Configuring a remote for a fork Syncing a fork git commit -m "Set SWARMLET flags to -DEDUK_MIN -

git commit -m "Set SWARMLET flags to -DEDUK_MIN -Os for testing purposes." userland / examples / duktape / Makefile git push git remote add upstream https: // github.com / helena-project / tock git remote -v git fetch upstream git checkout master gi

emote -v git fetch upstream git checkout master git merge upstream / master [$[Get Code]] See Also Notes for Tock Compilation - Compiling Duktape to TockOS Duktape2 Initial Compilation Duktape1 Hail - Main Hail page TockOS Edit - History - Print - Re
